### Post by Jbs63 on 2007-10-28
Ok i know this is an area that has been touched on quite a bit but i cant bet it to work. I want 1680x1050 but i can get 1400x1050 at best. i edited xorg.config but that diddnt do anything. any ideas?? my xorg config file is below (edited): it seems different to everone ellses. What do all the numbers mean?? :P

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"

	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"

EndSection

### Post by squaregoldfish on 2007-10-29
Try moving 1680x1050 to the front of the Modes line in the "Screen" section.
The Virtual line in the "Screen" section may also be causing your problem - try removing it.

Assuming your monitor is LCD, you could also try removing (commenting out) all lines in the "Screen" section except:

Identifier
Device
Defaultdepth

When I did this, X simply used the native resolution of the monitor.

Steve.

---
